faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Short answers for seed users who are ready to build.

items.question: Q: What is MonkeyUI?
items.answer: A: It extracts a "style DNA" from a UI reference you like (colors, typography scale, radius, shadows, spacing rhythm, component vibe), so AI-generated or AI-edited UI looks intentional and consistent, not like the generic default "AI look."

items.question: Q: What kind of reference do I need? Is one screenshot enough?
items.answer: A: One screenshot is enough to start. For best results, pick a high-quality screen that includes common components (buttons, forms, lists, cards). More coverage means a stronger signal and better consistency.

items.question: Q: Which IDEs are supported?
items.answer: A: Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and mainstream IDEs.

items.question: Q: Can it match the reference 100%?
items.answer: A: It is not aiming for pixel-perfect replication. The goal is consistent, product-quality style rules—type scale, color usage, radius/shadow intensity, and spacing density.

items.question: Q: Which stacks are supported (React/Vue/Tailwind/native)?
items.answer: A: Style DNA is framework-agnostic. You can map it to Tailwind tokens, CSS variables, or your component library theme.

items.question: Q: Why do I need to contact you for access?
items.answer: A: This is early access. We confirm your use case and expectations first, then grant access and collect feedback to iterate quickly.
